moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Add to QQQI and QQQ for increased income and broad index exposure.
Trim CRWD to reduce single-name high-beta exposure.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall exposure.
Focus on capital preservation and lower drawdown.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a ~1.5%/month return with a focus on capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration. Expect lower volatility and smaller drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged exposure and exit flagged positions.
Trim QQQI to rebalance towards growth.
Buy CRM and META, two high-scoring scanner setups, for quality tech exposure.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer and balanced risk profile.
Focus on risk-first growth with controlled leverage.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets a ~2.5%/month return, balancing growth with risk management. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while adding quality tech names. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to meet concentration limits.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit flagged positions and reallocate capital.
Trim QQQI to shift focus towards higher growth potential.
Buy CRM and ZS, top-rated scanner setups, for concentrated growth.
Add TECS, a leveraged ETF, to increase aggressive exposure.
Maintain a small cash buffer and utilize leverage for stretch growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets a ~4%/month return by leaning into high-scoring setups and leveraged ETFs. This approach significantly amplifies both potential upside and drawdown. Be aware of leveraged ETF decay and the potential for rapid, substantial losses if stops are not strictly adhered to.

plan: Trim SPY to meet concentration limits and reallocate.
Sell SOXL, SPAL, and CRWD to eliminate leveraged and high-beta exposure.
Trim SMH and QQQ to further reduce growth-oriented holdings.
Significantly add to QQQI, an income-generating ETF, for steady distributions.
Maintain a cash buffer for stability.
Focus on low drawdown and reliable monthly distributions.
income.read: This income plan targets a ~1%/month return by prioritizing steady monthly distributions and low drawdown. It significantly reduces growth-oriented and leveraged assets. While aiming for stability, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in market downturns.
